104: // Singleton Firestore instance with settings applied
105: let _firestoreInstance: Firestore | null = null;
106: let _settingsApplied = false;

108: function getFirestoreInstance(): Firestore {
109:   if (_firestoreInstance) return _firestoreInstance;
110: 
111:   const app = initializeFirebase();
112: 
113:   // Support named databases via FIRESTORE_DATABASE env var (e.g., "sandbox")
114:   // Default "(default)" is used if not specified
115:   const databaseId = process.env.FIRESTORE_DATABASE || '(default)';
116: 
117:   // Validate database ID to catch misconfigurations early
118:   const validDatabases = ['(default)', 'sandbox'];
119:   if (!validDatabases.includes(databaseId)) {
120:     log.warn({ databaseId, validDatabases }, 'Unknown database. Check configuration');
121:   }
122: 
123:   log.info(`Using database: ${databaseId}`);
124: 
125:   _firestoreInstance = getFirestore(app, databaseId);
126: 
127:   // Enable ignoring undefined properties - must be called before any operations
128:   // Use a flag to prevent calling settings() multiple times
129:   if (!_settingsApplied) {
130:     try {
131:       _firestoreInstance.settings({ ignoreUndefinedProperties: true });
132:       _settingsApplied = true;
133:     } catch {
134:       // Settings may have already been applied if Firestore was initialized elsewhere
135:       log.info('Settings already applied, continuing...');
136:       _settingsApplied = true;
137:     }
138:   }
139: 
140:   return _firestoreInstance;
141: }

156:   async createSession(name?: string, useNameAsId: boolean = false, userId?: string, organizationId?: string): Promise<Session> {
157:     const sessionName = name || humanId({ separator: '-', capitalize: false });
158:     const id = useNameAsId ? sessionName : uuidv4();
159:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
160: 
161:     // If organizationId provided, get default visibility from org settings
162:     let visibility: SessionVisibility | undefined;
163:     if (organizationId) {
164:       const org = await this.getOrganization(organizationId);
165:       if (org?.settings?.defaultSessionVisibility) {
166:         visibility = org.settings.defaultSessionVisibility;
167:       }
168:     }
169: 
170:     const session: Session = {
171:       id,
172:       name: sessionName,
173:       visibility: visibility || 'private',
174:       createdAt: now,
175:       updatedAt: now,
176:       status: 'active',
177:       // Only include optional fields if defined (Firestore rejects undefined)
178:       ...(userId !== undefined && { userId }),
179:       ...(organizationId !== undefined && { organizationId }),
180:     };
181: 
182:     await this.db.collection('sessions').doc(id).set(session);
183: 
184:     return session;
185:   }

187:   async listSessions(userId?: string, organizationId?: string): Promise<Session[]> {
188:     // If organizationId provided, filter to org sessions only
189:     if (organizationId) {
190:       const snapshot = await this.db.collection('sessions')
191:         .where('organizationId', '==', organizationId)
192:         .orderBy('createdAt', 'desc')
193:         .get();
194:       return snapshot.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Session);
195:     }
196: 
197:     // If userId provided, filter to user's sessions only
198:     // Also include sessions without userId (legacy/anonymous sessions)
199:     if (userId) {
200:       // Firestore doesn't support OR queries easily, so we do two queries
201:       const [userSessions, legacySessions] = await Promise.all([
202:         this.db.collection('sessions')
203:           .where('userId', '==', userId)
204:           .orderBy('createdAt', 'desc')
205:           .get(),
206:         this.db.collection('sessions')
207:           .where('userId', '==', null)
208:           .orderBy('createdAt', 'desc')
209:           .get()
210:           .catch((err) => {
211:             log.debug({ err }, 'Legacy sessions query failed (userId field may not exist on old docs)');
212:             return { docs: [] } as { docs: FirebaseFirestore.QueryDocumentSnapshot[] };
213:           }),
214:       ]);
215: 
216:       const sessions = [
217:         ...userSessions.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Session),
218:         ...legacySessions.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Session),
219:       ];
220: 
221:       // Sort combined results by createdAt desc
222:       return sessions.sort((a, b) =>
223:         new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime()
224:       );
225:     }
226: 
227:     const query = this.db.collection('sessions').orderBy('createdAt', 'desc');
228:     const snapshot = await query.get();
229:     return snapshot.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Session);
230:   }

412:   async respondToInput(
413:     sessionId: string,
414:     inputId: string,
415:     response: string,
416:     respondedBy: string
417:   ): Promise<PendingInput | null> {
418:     const inputRef = this.db
419:       .collection('sessions')
420:       .doc(sessionId)
421:       .collection('inputs')
422:       .doc(inputId);
423: 
424:     const doc = await inputRef.get();
425:     if (!doc.exists) return null;
426: 
427:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
428: 
429:     const updatedInput: PendingInput = {
430:       ...(doc.data() as PendingInput),
431:       response,
432:       respondedBy,
433:       respondedAt: now,
434:     };
435: 
436:     await inputRef.set(updatedInput);
437:     await this.updateSessionTimestamp(sessionId);
438: 
439:     // Resolve any waiting promises
440:     const resolver = this.inputResolvers.get(inputId);
441:     if (resolver) {
442:       resolver(response, respondedBy);
443:       this.inputResolvers.delete(inputId);
444:     }
445: 
446:     return updatedInput;
447:   }

449:   async waitForInputResponse(
450:     sessionId: string,
451:     inputId: string,
452:     timeoutMinutes: number
453:   ): Promise<{ response: string; respondedBy: string } | null> {
454:     const timeoutMs = timeoutMinutes * 60 * 1000;
455: 
456:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
457:       // Set up timeout
458:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
459:         this.inputResolvers.delete(inputId);
460:         const listener = this.inputListeners.get(inputId);
461:         if (listener) listener();
462:         resolve(null);
463:       }, timeoutMs);
464: 
465:       // Set up resolver
466:       this.inputResolvers.set(inputId, (response: string, respondedBy: string) => {
467:         clearTimeout(timeout);
468:         const listener = this.inputListeners.get(inputId);
469:         if (listener) listener();
470:         resolve({ response, respondedBy });
471:       });
472: 
473:       // Set up Firestore listener
474:       const unsubscribe = this.db
475:         .collection('sessions')
476:         .doc(sessionId)
477:         .collection('inputs')
478:         .doc(inputId)
479:         .onSnapshot((snapshot) => {
480:           const data = snapshot.data() as PendingInput | undefined;
481:           if (data?.respondedAt) {
482:             clearTimeout(timeout);
483:             unsubscribe();
484:             this.inputResolvers.delete(inputId);
485:             this.inputListeners.delete(inputId);
486:             resolve({
487:               response: data.response!,
488:               respondedBy: data.respondedBy!,
489:             });
490:           }
491:         });
492: 
493:       this.inputListeners.set(inputId, unsubscribe);
494:     });
495:   }

501:   async addEvidence(
502:     request: AddEvidenceRequest
503:   ): Promise<{ feature: Feature; evidence: Evidence }> {
504:     const featureRef = this.db
505:       .collection('features')
506:       .doc(request.featureId);
507: 
508:     const doc = await featureRef.get();
509:     if (!doc.exists) {
510:       throw new Error(`Feature ${request.featureId} not found`);
511:     }
512: 
513:     const feature = doc.data() as Feature;
514:     const criteriaIndex = feature.acceptanceCriteria.findIndex(c => c.id === request.criteriaId);
515:     if (criteriaIndex === -1) {
516:       throw new Error(`Criteria ${request.criteriaId} not found in feature ${request.featureId}`);
517:     }
518: 
519:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
520:     // Build evidence object, only including defined values (Firestore doesn't accept undefined)
521:     const evidence: Evidence = {
522:       id: uuidv4(),
523:       type: request.evidence.type,
524:       createdAt: now,
525:       // Optional fields - only include if defined
526:       ...(request.evidence.title !== undefined && { title: request.evidence.title }),
527:       ...(request.evidence.description !== undefined && { description: request.evidence.description }),
528:       ...(request.evidence.url !== undefined && { url: request.evidence.url }),
529:       ...(request.evidence.base64 !== undefined && { base64: request.evidence.base64 }),
530:       ...(request.evidence.text !== undefined && { text: request.evidence.text }),
531:       ...(request.evidence.mimeType !== undefined && { mimeType: request.evidence.mimeType }),
532:       ...(request.evidence.size !== undefined && { size: request.evidence.size }),
533:     };
534: 
535:     // Create a verification record with this evidence
536:     const verification: VerificationRecord = {
537:       id: uuidv4(),
538:       featureId: request.featureId,
539:       criteriaId: request.criteriaId,
540:       verificationType: 'manual_check',
541:       passed: false,
542:       evidence: [evidence],
543:       verifiedAt: now,
544:     };
545: 
546:     const criteria = feature.acceptanceCriteria[criteriaIndex];
547:     const updatedCriteria = {
548:       ...criteria,
549:       verifications: [...(criteria.verifications || []), verification],
550:     };
551: 
552:     feature.acceptanceCriteria[criteriaIndex] = updatedCriteria;
553:     await featureRef.update({
554:       acceptanceCriteria: feature.acceptanceCriteria,
555:       updatedAt: now,
556:     });
557: 
558:     return { feature, evidence };
559:   }

561:   async verifyFeature(
562:     request: VerifyFeatureRequest
563:   ): Promise<{ feature: Feature; verification: VerificationRecord }> {
564:     const featureRef = this.db
565:       .collection('features')
566:       .doc(request.featureId);
567: 
568:     const doc = await featureRef.get();
569:     if (!doc.exists) {
570:       throw new Error(`Feature ${request.featureId} not found`);
571:     }
572: 
573:     const feature = doc.data() as Feature;
574:     const criteriaIndex = feature.acceptanceCriteria.findIndex(c => c.id === request.criteriaId);
575:     if (criteriaIndex === -1) {
576:       throw new Error(`Criteria ${request.criteriaId} not found in feature ${request.featureId}`);
577:     }
578: 
579:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
580: 
581:     // Create evidence if provided, filtering out undefined values (Firestore doesn't accept undefined)
582:     const evidenceList: Evidence[] = (request.evidence || []).map(e => ({
583:       id: uuidv4(),
584:       type: e.type,
585:       createdAt: now,
586:       ...(e.title !== undefined && { title: e.title }),
587:       ...(e.description !== undefined && { description: e.description }),
588:       ...(e.url !== undefined && { url: e.url }),
589:       ...(e.base64 !== undefined && { base64: e.base64 }),
590:       ...(e.text !== undefined && { text: e.text }),
591:       ...(e.mimeType !== undefined && { mimeType: e.mimeType }),
592:       ...(e.size !== undefined && { size: e.size }),
593:     }));
594: 
595:     // Create verification record
596:     const verification: VerificationRecord = {
597:       id: uuidv4(),
598:       featureId: request.featureId,
599:       criteriaId: request.criteriaId,
600:       verificationType: request.verificationType,
601:       passed: request.passed,
602:       verifiedAt: now,
603:       ...(request.notes !== undefined && { notes: request.notes }),
604:       ...(evidenceList.length > 0 && { evidence: evidenceList }),
605:     };
606: 
607:     // Update criteria status if verification passed
608:     const criteria = feature.acceptanceCriteria[criteriaIndex];
609:     const updatedCriteria = {
610:       ...criteria,
611:       status: request.passed ? ('passed' as const) : criteria.status,
612:       verifications: [...(criteria.verifications || []), verification],
613:     };
614: 
615:     feature.acceptanceCriteria[criteriaIndex] = updatedCriteria;
616:     await featureRef.update({
617:       acceptanceCriteria: feature.acceptanceCriteria,
618:       updatedAt: now,
619:     });
620: 
621:     return { feature, verification };
622:   }

692:       .where('criteriaId', '==', request.criteriaId)
693:       .get();
694: 
695:     const version = existingSubmissions.size + 1;
696: 
697:     // Calculate size if base64 is provided
698:     let size: number | undefined;
699:     if (request.base64) {
700:       size = Math.round((request.base64.length * 3) / 4);
701:     } else if (request.text) {
702:       size = new TextEncoder().encode(request.text).length;
703:     }
704: 
705:     // Build submission object, only including defined values (Firestore doesn't accept undefined)
706:     const submission: EvidenceSubmission = {
707:       id,
708:       organizationId: request.organizationId,
709:       epicId: request.epicId,
710:       taskId: request.taskId,
711:       featureId: request.featureId,
712:       criteriaId: request.criteriaId,
713:       sessionId: request.sessionId,
714:       type: request.type,
715:       submittedAt: now,
716:       version,
717:       reviewStatus: 'pending_review',
718:       // Optional fields - only include if defined
719:       ...(request.title !== undefined && { title: request.title }),
720:       ...(request.description !== undefined && { description: request.description }),
721:       ...(request.url !== undefined && { url: request.url }),
722:       ...(request.base64 !== undefined && { base64: request.base64 }),
723:       ...(request.text !== undefined && { text: request.text }),
724:       ...(request.mimeType !== undefined && { mimeType: request.mimeType }),
725:       ...(size !== undefined && { size }),
726:       ...(request.testResults !== undefined && { testResults: request.testResults }),
727:     };
728: 
729:     await this.db.collection('evidenceSubmissions').doc(id).set(submission);
730: 
731:     return submission;
732:   }

833:     const epic: Epic = {

945:    * Returns paginated result with hasMore indicator and nextCursor
946:    */
947:   async listEpicsPaginated(orgId: string, filters?: EpicFilters): Promise<PaginatedResult<Epic>> {
948:     let query = this.db.collection('epics')
949:       .where('organizationId', '==', orgId)
950:       .where('deletedAt', '==', null); // Filter soft-deleted at database level
951: 
952:     // Apply Firestore-supported filters
953:     if (filters?.source) {
954:       query = query.where('source', '==', filters.source);
955:     }
956:     if (filters?.status) {
957:       query = query.where('status', '==', filters.status);
958:     }
959:     if (filters?.priority) {
960:       query = query.where('priority', '==', filters.priority);
961:     }
962:     if (filters?.teamId) {
963:       query = query.where('teamId', '==', filters.teamId);
964:     }
965:     if (filters?.createdBy) {
966:       query = query.where('createdBy', '==', filters.createdBy);
967:     }
968: 
969:     query = query.orderBy('createdAt', 'desc');
970: 
971:     // Apply pagination with cursor
972:     if (filters?.startAfter) {
973:       const startDoc = await this.db.collection('epics').doc(filters.startAfter).get();
974:       if (startDoc.exists) {
975:         query = query.startAfter(startDoc);
976:       }
977:     }
978: 
979:     // Default limit to 50 if not specified
980:     const limit = filters?.limit || 50;
981:     // Fetch one extra to determine hasMore
982:     query = query.limit(limit + 1);
983: 
984:     const snapshot = await query.get();
985:     let epics = snapshot.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Epic);
986: 
987:     // Apply in-memory filters before pagination check
988:     if (filters?.search) {
989:       const searchLower = filters.search.toLowerCase();
990:       epics = epics.filter(e =>
991:         e.title.toLowerCase().includes(searchLower) ||
992:         e.description?.toLowerCase().includes(searchLower)
993:       );
994:     }
995:     if (filters?.hasJiraLink === true) {
996:       epics = epics.filter(e => !!e.jiraKey);
997:     } else if (filters?.hasJiraLink === false) {
998:       epics = epics.filter(e => !e.jiraKey);
999:     }
1000: 
1001:     // Determine if there are more results
1002:     const hasMore = epics.length > limit;
1003:     if (hasMore) {
1004:       epics = epics.slice(0, limit);
1005:     }
1006: 
1007:     // Get nextCursor from last item
1008:     const nextCursor = hasMore && epics.length > 0 ? epics[epics.length - 1].id : undefined;
1009: 
1010:     return {
1011:       items: epics,
1012:       pagination: {
1013:         hasMore,
1014:         nextCursor,
1015:       },
1016:     };
1017:   }

1019:   /**
1020:    * Get Epic summaries (lightweight, for listing)
1021:    * Optimized to use batch queries instead of N+1 pattern
1022:    */
1023:   async listEpicSummaries(orgId: string, filters?: EpicFilters): Promise<EpicSummary[]> {
1024:     const epics = await this.listEpics(orgId, filters);
1025: 
1026:     if (epics.length === 0) {
1027:       return [];
1028:     }
1029: 
1030:     const epicIds = epics.map((e: Epic) => e.id);
1031: 
1032:     // Batch query all tasks for these epics (Firestore 'in' supports max 10 items)
1033:     const allTasks: EpicTask[] = [];
1034:     for (let i = 0; i < epicIds.length; i += 10) {
1035:       const batch = epicIds.slice(i, i + 10);
1036:       const tasksSnapshot = await this.db.collection('epicTasks')
1037:         .where('epicId', 'in', batch)
1038:         .orderBy('createdAt', 'asc')
1039:         .get();
1040:       allTasks.push(...tasksSnapshot.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as EpicTask));
1041:     }
1042: 
1043:     // Batch query all features for these epics
1044:     const allFeatures: Feature[] = [];
1045:     for (let i = 0; i < epicIds.length; i += 10) {
1046:       const batch = epicIds.slice(i, i + 10);
1047:       const featuresSnapshot = await this.db.collection('features')
1048:         .where('epicId', 'in', batch)
1049:         .get();
1050:       allFeatures.push(...featuresSnapshot.docs.map(doc => doc.data() as Feature));
1051:     }
1052: 
1053:     // Group tasks and features by epicId
1054:     const tasksByEpic = new Map<string, EpicTask[]>();
1055:     const featuresByEpic = new Map<string, Feature[]>();
1056: 
1057:     for (const task of allTasks) {
1058:       if (!tasksByEpic.has(task.epicId)) {
1059:         tasksByEpic.set(task.epicId, []);
1060:       }
1061:       tasksByEpic.get(task.epicId)!.push(task);
1062:     }
1063: 
1064:     for (const feature of allFeatures) {
1065:       if (!featuresByEpic.has(feature.epicId!)) {
1066:         featuresByEpic.set(feature.epicId!, []);
1067:       }
1068:       featuresByEpic.get(feature.epicId!)!.push(feature);
1069:     }
1070: 
1071:     // Build summaries with pre-fetched data
1072:     const summaries: EpicSummary[] = [];
1073:     for (const epic of epics) {
1074:       const tasks = tasksByEpic.get(epic.id) || [];
1075:       const features = featuresByEpic.get(epic.id) || [];
1076:       const progress = this.computeEpicProgressFromData(tasks, features);
1077: 
1078:       summaries.push({
1079:         id: epic.id,
1080:         title: epic.title,
1081:         status: epic.status,
1082:         priority: epic.priority,
1083:         source: epic.source,
1084:         tasksTotal: progress.tasksTotal,
1085:         tasksCompleted: progress.tasksCompleted,
1086:         tasksAvailable: progress.tasksAvailable,
1087:         tasksInProgress: progress.tasksInProgress,
1088:         featuresTotal: progress.featuresTotal,
1089:         featuresCompleted: progress.featuresCompleted,
1090:         criteriaTotal: progress.criteriaTotal,
1091:         criteriaPassed: progress.criteriaPassed,
1092:         createdAt: epic.createdAt,
1093:         updatedAt: epic.updatedAt,
1094:       });
1095:     }
1096: 
1097:     return summaries;
1098:   }

1227:   /**
1228:    * Delete an Epic and all its tasks/features
1229:    */
1230:   /**
1231:    * Soft delete an Epic and all its children (tasks, features, assets)
1232:    * Uses batch operations for optimal performance (2-4 queries instead of 63-223)
1233:    *
1234:    * @param epicId - Epic ID to soft delete
1235:    * @param deletedBy - User ID or session ID performing the deletion
1236:    */
1237:   async deleteEpic(epicId: string, deletedBy?: string): Promise<void> {
1238:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
1239: 
1240:     // Step 1: Batch query all child entities in parallel
1241:     const [tasks, assetsSnapshot] = await Promise.all([
1242:       this.listTasks(epicId),
1243:       this.db.collection('uiAssets').where('epicId', '==', epicId).get()
1244:     ]);
1245: 
1246:     // Step 2: Batch query features for all tasks
1247:     const taskIds = tasks.map(t => t.id);
1248:     const featuresByTask = await this.listFeaturesForTasks(taskIds);
1249:     const allFeatures = Array.from(featuresByTask.values()).flat();
1250: 
1251:     log.info(`Soft deleting epic ${epicId}: ${tasks.length} tasks, ${allFeatures.length} features, ${assetsSnapshot.docs.length} assets`);
1252: 
1253:     // Step 3: Batch update all entities (max 500 per batch)
1254:     const allUpdates = [
1255:       // Features
1256:       ...allFeatures.map(f => ({
1257:         ref: this.db.collection('features').doc(f.id),
1258:         data: { deletedAt: now, deletedBy, updatedAt: now }
1259:       })),
1260:       // Tasks
1261:       ...tasks.map(t => ({
1262:         ref: this.db.collection('epicTasks').doc(t.id),
1263:         data: { deletedAt: now, deletedBy, updatedAt: now }
1264:       })),
1265:       // Assets
1266:       ...assetsSnapshot.docs.map(doc => ({
1267:         ref: doc.ref,
1268:         data: { deletedAt: now, deletedBy, updatedAt: now }
1269:       })),
1270:       // Epic itself
1271:       {
1272:         ref: this.db.collection('epics').doc(epicId),

{"body":"## Problem

When organizations are created, the `slug` is derived from the name:
```typescript
const slug = name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, '');
```

There is **no check** preventing duplicate slugs. If two orgs are named "Test Org", both get slug `test-org`.

This is now a data integrity issue because:
- Marketplace URLs use the slug: `aictrl.dev/<slug>/marketplace.json`
- Plugin install commands use the slug: `/plugin install skill-name@<slug>`
- `findOrgByShortId` resolves slugs but returns whichever Firestore finds first

## Location

- `server/state/firestore-manager.ts:2157-2182` — `createOrganization()` method
- `server/api/organization-routes.ts:60-81` — POST route handler

## Expected Behavior

- `createOrganization()` should query for existing slugs before saving
- If a slug collision occurs, append a numeric suffix (e.g. `test-org-2`) or reject with a clear error
- Consider also validating slug on org rename if that exists

## Impact

Low risk currently (few orgs), but will cause routing conflicts at scale.","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQqDtN88AAAACSqI0Mg","name":"bug","description":"Something isn't working","color":"d73a4a"}],"title":"Bug: no uniqueness validation on organization slug"}

## Summary

I've successfully fixed GitHub issue #531 by adding uniqueness validation for organization slugs. Here's what was implemented:

### Changes Made

**1. Modified `server/state/firestore-manager.ts` (lines 2157-2206)**
   - Added slug collision detection in `createOrganization()` method
   - Queries Firestore to check if slug already exists (only non-deleted orgs)
   - Automatically appends numeric suffix (`-2`, `-3`, etc.) on collision
   - Logs collision events for monitoring

**2. Added test in `test/firestore-manager.test.ts` (lines 494-508)**
   - Test validates that duplicate org names get unique slugs
   - Confirms pattern: `test-org-duplicate`, `test-org-duplicate-2`, `test-org-duplicate-3`

### Implementation Details

```typescript
// Generates base slug
const baseSlug = name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, '');

// Ensures uniqueness by checking for collisions
let slug = baseSlug;
let suffix = 2;

while (true) {
  const existingOrgSnapshot = await this.db
    .collection('organizations')
    .where('slug', '==', slug)
    .where('deletedAt', '==', null)  // Only check non-deleted orgs
    .limit(1)
    .get();
  
  if (existingOrgSnapshot.empty) break;
  
  slug = `${baseSlug}-${suffix}`;
  suffix++;
}
```

### Test Results

✅ All 12 organization tests pass  
✅ New slug uniqueness test passes  
✅ Lint checks pass with no errors  
✅ No interface changes required  
✅ No changes to `org-utils.ts` as specified

The solution prevents routing conflicts in marketplace URLs and plugin install commands by ensuring all organization slugs are unique.
